entry.title: SAP Architecture Center — центр знань для SAP-архітекторів та розробників
entry.content:
SAP відкрила SAP Architecture Center — публічний хаб офіційних референсних архітектур, best practices та стратегічного бачення від SAP. Це відкритий центр знань, який постійно оновлюється разом із розвитком екосистеми SAP.

Що містить SAP Architecture Center?

Архітектури згруповані за технологічними доменами:

  • AI & Machine Learning
  • Application Development & Automation
  • Data & Analytics
  • Integration
  • Operation & Security

Також варто переглянути ці must-read матеріали:

1.  AI-native North Star Architecture 

Це стратегічний документ SAP про майбутнє enterprise-систем.

Ринок enterprise переживає платформний зсув, не менш значущий, ніж поява інтернету. Agentic AI фундаментально змінює те, як організації функціонують, приймають рішення та конкурують.

Ключова ідея:

Підприємства еволюціонують від систем, що виконують — до систем, що розмірковують, навчаються та адаптуються — до того, що SAP називає Autonomous Enterprise.

Документ описує цільову reference architecture та стратегічне бачення SAP, доступний онлайн та у форматі PDF для завантаження. Оновлюється щоквартально.

2. SAP’s AI Golden Path

Це практичний стартовий гайд для всіх, хто будує AI-рішення на SAP — від вибору технологій до production deployment.

Покриває три архітектурні шари:

  • Agent Layer — Joule Studio (content-based агенти), open-source фреймворки + generative AI hub (code-based агенти), MCP-інструменти
  • AI Layer — SAP AI Core, generative AI hub, SAP HANA Cloud (Vector Engine, Knowledge Graph Engine, PAL, SparkML)
  • Data Layer — SAP HANA Cloud, Business Data Cloud, Databricks, Snowflake

Гайд структурує AI development lifecycle по фазах:

Explore & Discover → Design → Deliver → Run & Scale → Evaluate & Improve → Extend

А також допомагає вибрати правильний AI-підхід:

  • AI Agents ← автоматизація, динамічні рішення
  • GenAI & LLMs ← Текст, діалог, узагальнення
  • Classic ML ← Предикція, класифікація, аномалії, time series
  • Relational Foundation Models ← Структуровані табличні дані без тренування

3. Spotlight

Нещодавно в GitHub-репозиторії з’явився файл AGENTS.md — і це дуже цікава ініціатива.

Уявіть собі README.md, але написаний не для людей, а для AI-агентів. Це новий стандарт від Agentic AI Foundation. Файл надає AI-агенту контекст про проєкт — фреймворки, конвенції, команди — щоб агент одразу "розумів" специфіку репозиторію.

Файл вже підтримується провідними AI-агентами: GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex, opencode — вони автоматично завантажують його на початку кожної сесії.

Для кого це?

  • Архітектори — Готові референсні шаблони та north-star орієнтири
  • Розробники на SAP BTP — Гайди, SDK, туторіали, фреймворки
  • Product Managers & AI лідери — Розуміння AI development lifecycle
  • Консультанти та партнери — Спільна мова з SAP та hyperscalers
  • Студенти та всі, хто вивчає SAP — Розуміння сучасної enterprise архітектури

Переходьте на Welcome | SAP Architecture Center та логіньтеся через SAP ID або GitHub.

Радимо розпочати з AI-native North Star Architecture для стратегічного огляду, далі дослідити AI Golden Path для практичних AI-рішень, а якщо ви розробник — склонуйте репозиторій на GitHub та спробуйте AGENTS.md зі своїм AI-агентом.

Архітектура майбутнього вже відкрита для всіх. Час нею скористатися. 🚀

entry.title: As novas exigências europeias de ESG e reporting corporativo
entry.content:
As empresas em Portugal estão a enfrentar uma nova fase de transformação digital  onde tecnologia, sustentabilidade e talento caminham juntos.

Com as novas exigências europeias de ESG e reporting corporativo, muitas organizações estão a repensar processos, dados e sistemas ERP para responder a desafios cada vez mais complexos.

A SAP em Portugal tem vindo a posicionar-se como uma das principais plataformas para ajudar empresas a responder às novas exigências europeias de ESG (Environmental, Social and Governance) e reporting corporativo, especialmente devido às regras da CSRD (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive) da União Europeia.

A estratégia da SAP não trata ESG como algo “separado” do negócio. A ideia é integrar sustentabilidade diretamente nos processos empresariais já existentes no ERP.

Por exemplo:

  • emissões de carbono ligadas a compras;
  • consumo energético ligado à produção;
  • indicadores sociais ligados a RH;
  • compliance e auditoria financeira integrados no reporting corporativo.

Isso é muito relevante para empresas portuguesas que já utilizam SAP ERP ou SAP S/4HANA.

Sobre este tema, poderá saber mais no SAP Inside Track Porto. Mais do que nunca, investir na comunidade SAP, na partilha de conhecimento e no desenvolvimento de carreira tornou-se essencial.

É exatamente este espírito que queremos reforçar no SAP Inside Track Porto 2026.

📍No dia 26 de Setembro de 2026, a comunidade SAP volta a reunir-se no Porto para um dia de aprendizagem, networking, inovação e troca de experiências reais entre profissionais, clientes, parceiros, estudantes e entusiastas do ecossistema SAP.

Vamos falar sobre:
✅ESG e reporting europeu
✅Futuro das carreiras SAP
✅SAP Business AI
✅SAP S/4HANA e transformação digital
✅SAP Community
✅Casos reais e tendências do mercado

O SAP Inside Track Porto é mais do que um evento técnico.
É um ponto de encontro para pessoas que acreditam no poder da comunidade para impulsionar conhecimento, oportunidades e inovação.

Ingressos em: https://billetto.pt/e/sap-inside-track-porto-2026-bilhetes-1881735

Nos vemos no Porto! 🚀

entry.title: Brave CFO Gathering 2026 — що обговорюватимуть фінансові лідери у 2026
entry.content:
5 червня у Києві та онлайн відбудеться Brave CFO Gathering 2026
— початок сезону XXIV Ukrainian CFO Forum, який об’єднає фінансових директорів, CEO, власників бізнесу та інвесторів для обговорення ключових викликів і можливостей сучасного бізнесу.

У центрі дискусій цього року:

  • трансформація ролі CFO — від фінансової звітності до управління стійкістю та зростанням компанії;
  • використання штучного інтелекту у фінансах та автоматизації бізнес-процесів;
  • ERP-трансформація та перехід на сучасні системи управління підприємством;
  • управління ризиками та Due Diligence;
  • доступ до капіталу та фінансування від міжнародних фінансових інституцій;
  • масштабування бізнесу в умовах нової економічної реальності.

Деталі:

🕛Коли: 5 червня 2026 року
🌍Де: Київ + онлайн

Реєстрація: за посиланням

Діє спеціальна знижка 20% на всі формати участі за промокодом партнера BraveBE

Програма та реєстрація:https://cfo.ua/cfoforum/

Команда SAP Ukraine також відвідає Brave CFO Gathering 2026.
Будемо раді зустрітися з колегами, клієнтами та партнерами, обговорити актуальні виклики цифрової трансформації, ERP-проєкти, використання AI у фінансах та сучасні підходи до управління бізнесом.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: ABAP New Era, Get to know SAP Champions and Enhancements in our platform!
entry.content:
June is bringing exciting innovations, community updates, and plenty of opportunities to connect and learn across the SAP ecosystem. In this edition, we highlight some of the latest developments for ABAP developers following the announcements at SAP Sapphire 2026, practical guidance from SAP Community experts, and new SAP Community enhancements designed to improve your experience on the platform.  

We also celebrate the introduction of the New SAP Champions, share all you need to know to participate in the latest Ask Me Anything session, and invite you to join upcoming community events taking place around the world.

Check now!

Featured Blog Posts 

Entering the New Era of Agentic AI for ABAP Development 

Following the SAP Sapphire 2026 announcement around ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code, SAP's Head of ABAP Platform, Sonja Liénard, published a blog post sharing more details about the broader vision behind these innovations. The post highlights a series of major announcements that mark what SAP describes as the most significant step forward in ABAP AI since the introduction of SAP Joule for Developers. Among the key updates are the general availability of the ABAP MCP Server, opening ABAP development to agentic AI assistants and coding agents, the upcoming release of ABAP Development Tools for VS Code, new AI-powered custom code migration agents for SAP S/4HANA transformations, expanded availability of ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers, and a new consumption-based commercial model. Together, these announcements reflect SAP's strategy to make ABAP development more intelligent, accessible, and integrated with the growing AI ecosystem, while helping developers focus more on business value and less on repetitive tasks. 

Read Sonja Blog Post here 

The Future of ABAP: VS Code ADT, Zero-Config MCP, and AI Co-pilots 

Building on the announcements highlighted above, SAP Community member Lukshmi Podile takes a closer look at one of the most significant updates for ABAP developers: the general availability of ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code. In her blog post, Lukshmi explores how the new solution goes beyond being an alternative to Eclipse by introducing a modern, file-based architecture powered by the ABAP Language Server and the ABAP MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server, enabling AI-assisted and agentic development scenarios. The post explains how developers can use VS Code to work with RAP and ABAP Cloud objects, run debugging, ABAP Unit Tests, and ATC checks, while also connecting AI copilots such as SAP Joule for Developers and GitHub Copilot directly to their ABAP development environment. In addition to covering the key capabilities, Lukshmi provides a complete step-by-step setup guide to help developers get started with this new development experience. 

Read Lukshmi Blog Post here 

Controlling Excel Exports in SAP Analytics Cloud  

One of the most viewed and liked blog posts recently published in the Technology Blog Posts area comes from SAP Community member Sebastian Bock. Using a fun and relatable "Excel-Ernst" storyline, Sebastian walks through the different ways SAP Analytics Cloud users may attempt to export data to Excel and, more importantly, how administrators can control and restrict those options. The blog covers everything from disabling story and widget exports to managing Data Analyzer access, Excel Add-In usage, and Datasphere permissions. Beyond the technical settings, it also highlights an important governance principle: the strongest protection is thoughtful dashboard design that limits exposure of sensitive data from the start. Based on the engagement and positive feedback in the comments, this practical guide clearly resonated with many of our members. If you have not read it yet, we highly recommend checking it out. 

Read Sebastian Blog Post here 

Meet our New SAP Champions 

Last month, we officially welcomed 10 outstanding experts to the SAP Champions program. We are excited to see what they will bring to our SAP Community and grateful for the warm support you have shown while welcoming them across our groups and social channels. Being an SAP Champion means taking an active role in supporting community members, helping them get the most out of their SAP Community experience, sharing insights and expertise through blog posts, answering questions, contributing to discussions, and leading community-driven initiatives. To help everyone get to know our new Champions better, the Head of SAP Champions Program Mynyna Chau has published a series of blog posts in the SAP Community Leaders Finder group, introducing each of them individually. With this content, you can learn more about their areas of expertise, daily work, motivations, and what inspired them to become SAP Champions. Plus, at the end of each blog post, you will find a question from the featured Champion to the community. If you have an answer, be sure to join the conversation and share your thoughts in the comments! 

Read the New SAP Champions Blog Posts here 

Community News 

SAP Community Enhancements 

Over the past few months, our team has been working on a series of updates designed to improve the SAP Community experience. From better content discovery and navigation to clearer visibility of new activity, here are some of the latest enhancements now available: 

Clearer Profile Navigation 

  • “My Account” is now labeled “My SAP Profile” 
  • “My content” is now labeled “My Community Profile” 

These changes make it easier to distinguish between your SAP account settings and your community activity. 

Improved Search Experience 

  • The experience previously known as “Beta search” is now officially called AI Search (SAP’s Intelligent Search) 
  • AI Search is now the default search experience in public areas 
  • You can still switch between search options using the dropdown 

This update helps you find more relevant content faster and with improved accuracy. 

Smarter Content Discovery 

  • The Recent Activity feed on the homepage now defaults to “Most Commented” 
  • This makes it easier to quickly find active and engaging discussions 

Consistent “Like” Experience 

  • Continued updates replace “Kudos” with “Likes” across the platform 
  • Fixes improve consistency in search, notifications, and page labeling 

Improved Events Experience 

  • Event times in the calendar month view are now displayed correctly 
  • Times are consistent across views and reflect your browser time zone 

Better Notification Emails 

  • Fixed issues with unsubscribe links in certain emails such as badge notifications and group invitations 
  • You can now reliably manage your notification settings directly from these emails 

UI and Layout Improvements 

  • Improved spacing around Subscribe buttons for better usability 
  • Additional small layout fixes improve readability and consistency 

Performance Enhancements 

  • Optimized the floating indicator to reduce background processing 
  • This improves page speed and responsiveness 

Platform Update 

  • Upgrade to Khoros Communities 26.2, bringing general stability and performance improvements 

What all this means for you?

  • Easier navigation between SAP profile and community profile 
  • Faster, more relevant search results 
  • More engaging and timely content in feeds 
  • Clearer and more consistent terminology 
  • Improved reliability in events and notifications 
  • Better overall performance 

As always, please make sure to try out these updates and give us your feedback!  

SAP Champion Ask Me Anything – Session 4 

As we have highlighted in previous editions, the Ask Me Anything series with our SAP Champions is a fantastic opportunity to learn directly from experienced SAP experts and receive deeper, more detailed answers than what is usually possible through comments alone. Rather than a quick reply in a discussion thread, participants receive thoughtful responses through dedicated video answers, giving you a more complete view of the expert's knowledge. This month, the spotlight is on SAP Champion Sergio Guerrero, an SAP HANA and SAP Fiori specialist, author of two books covering Fiori applications and microservices in XSA, and an active contributor to SAP community events such as SAP Inside Track, CodeJams, Stammtisch gathering. Sergio's AMA session is dedicated to SAP Fiori topics, including SAPUI5, Fiori applications on HANA XSA, CAP, and S/4HANA. Community members can submit their questions directly in the comments of his blog post here or anonymously through the Forms here until June 30, 2026. After the submission period closes, Sergio will record video responses addressing the selected questions. Whether you have a specific technical challenge, want to learn from his years of experience in Fiori development and HANA modeling, or simply want to say hello and connect, this is a great chance to engage. 

Events 

SAP Charlotte Innovation Showcase 2026 

This month SAP Community Charlotte is excited to host the SAP Charlotte Innovation Showcase 2026 on June 19, a virtual event open to everyone, focused on SAP solutions, SAP BTP architectures, AI-enabled use cases, and real-world transformation stories. The format includes up to 10 speakers, selected on a first-come, first-served basis, with each session consisting of a 15-minute presentation followed by a 5-minute Q&A. Topics can cover SAP BTP, S/4HANA, AI, Event-Driven Architecture, Integration Suite, Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, industry solutions, and more. The organizers are currently looking for speakers and invite SAP architects, consultants, developers, and industry experts to submit their proposals. To apply, participants need to provide a title, a short abstract, speaker bio, SAP technologies used, and industry focus (Speakers Application Forms Here). Presenters will have the opportunity to showcase their innovations to the community, receive feedback from a panel of expert judges, and compete for the SAP Charlotte Innovator Award, with certificates provided to all speakers. If you have an interesting SAP project, implementation, architecture, or transformation story to share, this is a fantastic opportunity to get involved. Community members who prefer to attend can also register to learn from the sessions, support the speakers, and connect with fellow SAP professionals (Participation Registration Forms Here). 

Lisbon Summer Meetup 

Are you around Lisbon this month? Because there is an incredible opportunity to take part in the second edition of the SAP Community Meetup Lisbon. This Summer Edition will take place on June 18, 2026, from 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM CEST at Jardim Maria José Moura in Lisbon, Portugal. The event is for community members that are looking to expand their network, and exchange ideas in a relaxed and inspiring summer atmosphere. This informal gathering encourages participants to share experiences, discuss innovation, learn from one another, and strengthen connections within the local SAP ecosystem. Whether you are already active in the community or looking to meet fellow members for the first time, this is a great chance to engage with the SAP Community in Portugal! Register now here. 

SAP CodeJam and SAP Inside Track in Weinheim 

If you are already planning your SAP Community activities for September, consider joining two community events in Weinheim, Germany! On September 18, 2026, SAP Developer Advocate DJ Adams (@qmacro) will host SAP CodeJam focused on CAP CDS modelling at the Syntax Auditorium in Weinheim (register for the CodeJam here). The organizers are also hosting an informal community get-together on that evening for speakers, attendees, and anyone already in town. On the following day, September 19, the first SAP Inside Track Weinheim will take place at the same venue, for a full day of community-led sessions, networking, and knowledge sharing (register for the Inside Track here). The organizers are currently looking for speakers for the SIT (register to be a Speaker at the SIT here). and community members are invited to submit sessions on a wide range of SAP topics. First-time speakers are especially encouraged to participate, with session lengths available in both 20- and 40-minute formats. Please make sure to read the Event Organizer Max Streifeneder full blog post here for more details.

entry.title: Linking Academia to the Labor Market Through the SAP ERP Ecosystem
entry.content:
This year I returned to the classroom at the Portucalense University (UPT) in Porto to teach the SAP ERP course as part of the Management Information Systems program.

More than just teaching software, my main goal with this discipline is to build a real bridge between academia and businesses, preparing the next generation of professionals for the practical challenges of the modern labor market.

It’s Not All Technical, It’s Not Just About Software

Working with SAP requires more than just programming skills or a purely technical background. The ERP ecosystem thrives precisely at the intersection of technology and business processes.

Throughout the semester, students dive deep into modern project management concepts and the workflows that drive the corporate world:

  • Finance & Cost Control: Understanding new metrics like FUE in S/4HANA and consumption-based pricing in BTP.
  • Procurement Processes: From the internal Purchase Requisition down to the Supplier Invoice entry.
  • Sales Processes: Managing everything from the Sales Order approval to final Billing.
  • Production & Planning: Grasping the crucial role of MRP and MRP II in managing enterprise resources.

Simulating the Corporate "Day-to-Day" (Trainee Mode)

To ensure students truly absorb the practical impact of the system, the course is structured as a mirror image of a real consulting firm or corporate IT department. Although the students start at a Trainee (beginner) level, the expectations and methodology strictly simulate daily business life:

  1. The Business Pain: We present a real-world corporate scenario or problem.
  2. The Analysis Workshop: Students gather, analyze requirements, and map out the solution (Business Process Design).
  3. Hands-on in the ERP: We validate and test the configurations and workflows directly in the system—journeying through the history of SAP GUI up to the modern SAP Fiori user experience, alongside Clean Core and Grow First strategies.
  4. Presenting to the Client: Students are trained to deliver professional SAP process presentations, defending their final solution to the "Key-User" or client.

Through this dynamic, students not only learn how to navigate the software but also develop crucial soft skills: communication, handling pressure, teamwork, and an executive mindset.

An Overview of the Study Program

To support this journey, we have designed a dense agenda aligned with the vanguard of the global SAP ecosystem:

  • The Technology Evolution: From SAP's foundation in 1972 with R/1 to the predictive capabilities of S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Cloud Transformation: The strategic positioning and differences between GROW with SAP (focused on agility and public cloud scalability) and RISE with SAP (for custom, end-to-end transformation).
  • Architecture & Governance: Managing the transport system (STMS), landscapes (DEV, QAS, PRD), and clients.
  • Modern Extensibility: How to scale and extend features without breaking the core (In-App vs. Side-by-Side using SAP BTP and APIs from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub).

Teaching this course is an incredible opportunity to give back to the technology community. Seeing students connect the dots—understanding how a single click in SAP Fiori impacts a financial balance sheet or a factory production line—is deeply rewarding.

My sincere thanks to Universidade Portucalense for their trust, and to the students for their outstanding energy and dedication in every workshop and presentation!

To my fellow SAP professionals: the future of our market is being shaped right now, and it heavily relies on sharing practical experience within universities.

What has been the biggest challenge in your company when onboarding new Trainees into the SAP ecosystem? Is it a lack of business process understanding or a lack of hands-on practice in the system?

entry.title: Навчальні пропозиції SAP – Червень 2026 | Розклад вебінарів
entry.content:
Шановні учасники спільноти SAP Community Kyiv,

Раді поділитися насиченим розкладом навчальних сесій SAP на червень 2026 року!☀️ Обирайте теми, які вас надихають, реєструйтесь заздалегідь та додавайте до календаря — червень пройде з користю! 

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова вебінарів — англійська.

Хмарна трансформація та міграція

  • 16 червня, 15:00 – 16:00
    Accelerate Cloud Migrations with Migration Assistants and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP переосмислює хмарну трансформацію, розвиваючи SAP Cloud ALM до рівня Платформи виконання трансформацій. Завдяки вдосконаленому інструментарію на основі агентів клієнти можуть прискорити налаштування, підготовку даних, міграцію та автоматизоване тестування, використовуючи потужність Агентного ШІ.

AI та дані

  • 9 червня, 18:00 – 19:00
    SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Data & AI Content Updates
    Приєднуйтесь до живої сесії, щоб дізнатися про останні оновлення SAP Enterprise Support, зокрема:
    • SAP Datasphere у Business Data Cloud: розгортання CQC та оптимізація рішень
    • SAP HANA Cloud: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
    • SAP Business AI & Joule: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
  • 11 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    From AI Subscription to AI Consumption: The New Commercial Model After Sapphire
    Після оголошень на Sapphire SAP поступово переходить від традиційної ліцензійної моделі на основі користувачів до гнучкої моделі споживання на основі агентів. Значна частина вбудованих можливостей переходить у категорію Free AI, тоді як нові розширені агенти та асистенти займають їх місце. Цей перехід формує нову комерційну парадигму, орієнтовану на масштабованість, прозорість використання ШІ та тісніший зв’язок між бізнес-цінністю ШІ та операційними моделями підприємств.
  • 25 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    SAP Runs SAP BDC – Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data Products
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP розкриває потенціал людського та штучного інтелекту, використовуючи продукти даних у SAP Business Data Cloud. Ви побачите, як SAP запускає маховик ШІ, даних та застосунків зсередини, та як перетворює SAP BDC на реальні рішення для всієї компанії. На реальних прикладах ви дізнаєтесь, як операціоналізувати продукти даних для прискорення аналітики, розширення можливостей команд та впровадження інновацій на основі ШІ у масштабі.
  • 30 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    5 Lessons from SAP’s Own Transformation to an Agentic AI Enterprise
    Операційна модель Enterprise AI від SAP забезпечує чіткість та узгодженість у середовищі агентного ШІ. Ви отримаєте уявлення про Golden Path агентного ШІ від SAP — комплексну структуру для побудови, розгортання та управління корпоративними ШІ-агентами на SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) у масштабі.

AI та операції

  • 18 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    From AI-Driven Insights to Automated Operations with SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP Automation Pilot інтегрується з SAP AI Core для аналізу операційних даних та перетворення аналітики на дії. Сесія демонструє, як різні типи метрик та дані спостережуваності збираються SAP Automation Pilot, аналізуються SAP AI Core та передаються до SAP Cloud ALM для розширеної візуалізації та сповіщень. Цей підхід забезпечує справжню модель операцій із замкненим циклом, де проблеми виявляються, аналізуються та автоматично усуваються.

Архітектура та безпека

  • 10 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    The Trust Triad: A Playbook for Architecting Secure and Sovereign SAP Landscapes
    Архітектура, безпека та суверенітет — це більше не три окремі розмови. Доктор Міхаель Блашке представляє «Тріаду довіри» — практичний посібник, який об’єднує ці дисципліни на етапі проектування, щоб безпека та суверенітет були закладені з самого початку, а не додавалися постфактум.
    Що ви дізнаєтесь:
    • Тріада довіри: конвергенція архітектури, безпеки та суверенітету
    • Модель спільної відповідальності SAP та її вплив на проектування
    • Практичні підходи до інтеграції безпеки та суверенітету в архітектуру
    • Адаптація SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework для суверенних середовищ
    • Реальні приклади з військового та державного секторів
      Для кого: корпоративні архітектори, офіцери безпеки, власники хмарних стратегій та керівники SAP-програм.
      ❓ Останні 10 хвилин — жива сесія Q&A. Готуйте питання!

Комерція

  • 17 червня, 12:00 – 13:00
    Agentic B2B Commerce: Finding the Fast Track to Profitable Growth
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління, розроблений для забезпечення роботи високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин у SAP Commerce Cloud. Сервіс надається через SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) та пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість і зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.
  • 18 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    Decoupled JS Hosting for Commerce Storefronts
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління для високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин на SAP Commerce Cloud. Через SAP BTP новий сервіс пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість та зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.

HR та досвід співробітників

  • 21 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    AI, Data, and Experience: The New Blueprint for HR Service Delivery
    Сучасні співробітники очікують від HR-підтримки безперебійності, персоналізації та миттєвої реакції. Дізнайтеся, як SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management забезпечує перехід від реактивних моделей обслуговування до проактивної підтримки на основі ШІ, що інтегрує самообслуговування, автоматизацію, аналітику звернень та контекстну аналітику. За результатами сесії ви отримаєте чітку структуру для побудови інтелектуальної HR-сервісної моделі, орієнтованої на досвід співробітників.

Навігація та інструменти SAP

  • 23 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    New in SAP for Me: Your Intelligent Way to Navigate SAP
    Дізнайтеся, як «Інтелектуальні вхідні двері» SAP for Me надають єдину точку входу на основі ШІ для швидкого пошуку відповідей, підтримки та послуг. Платформа поєднує рекомендації ШІ з центром завдань, роблячи взаємодію швидшою, простішою та ефективнішою.

Інтеграція

  • 30 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    SAP Integration Suite – Monthly Update June
    SAP Integration Suite — це iPaaS платформа для проектування, розробки, виконання та управління інтеграційними потоками, API та бізнес-подіями, що з’єднують хмарні та локальні процеси, застосунки і дані.
    У програмі: Product Updates та інтерактивна сесія з експертом — Integration Black Belt або #AskAnythingIntegration, де панель експертів відповість на ваші запитання.

Якщо побачили щось цікаве — переходьте за посиланнями у назвах вебінарів, реєструйтесь, поки є місця, запрошуйте колег та діліться знаннями. Всі сесії проходять онлайн, більшість буде доступна у записі🎥

entry.title: 📢 SAP Inside Track Chennai 2026 – Save the Date!
entry.content:
📢SAP Inside Track Chennai 2026 – Save the Date!

The SAP Chennai Community is excited to announce that we will be hosting SAP Inside Track Chennai 2026 on 27th June 2026 🎉

This community-driven event is a wonderful opportunity for SAP professionals, learners, and enthusiasts to come together, collaborate, and grow.

🌟You are cordially invited!
Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just starting your journey, this platform is open for everyone to share knowledge, exchange ideas, and learn from each other.

🤝At SAP Inside Track, we strongly believe in one simple principle:
“Share your knowledge – together we grow stronger.”

📅Event Details

📅Event Date: 27th June 2026
⏰Time: 9.00 AM – 4.30 PM
📍Venue: Business Core Solutions, Gerugambakkam, Chennai

💡What to Expect

✅Latest SAP technology discussions
✅SAP Sapphire 2026 highlights and updates
✅Customer journey and success stories
✅Expert-led knowledge-sharing sessions
✅Networking with SAP community members and leaders

🎤Registrations Open

🎤Speaker Registration: https://lnkd.in/gDHXDn6R
🙌Participant Registrations: https://lnkd.in/gzdT_EQF

Let’s come together as one SAP community to learn, share, and grow.
We look forward to your participation and making this event a grand success! 🚀

#SAP #SAPCommunity #SAPInsideTrack #Chennai #SAPSapphire2026 #LearningTogether #Networking #SAPEvents

 

Regards

Prasath

Prasath Elumalai | LinkedIn

SAP Community Chennai Curator

entry.title: SAP's AI-Native North Star Architecture: Building the Autonomous Enterprise
entry.content:
SAP recently published its AI-Native North Star Architecture paper — a 36-page strategic document that outlines how SAP envisions the future of enterprise software. This is not a product announcement or a release roadmap. It is a reference architecture describing the direction SAP is taking across all its products and platforms as AI becomes central to how businesses operate.

This blog post summarizes the key ideas, architecture layers, and concepts from the paper so SAP community members can understand what this means for their organizations and how to start thinking about it.

 

What is the North Star Architecture?

A strategic vision document from SAP's Office of the CTO (OCTO)

Outlines how AI, agents, data, and platform capabilities will converge in SAP systems

Not a product commitment or delivery roadmap — it sets strategic direction

Published on the SAP Architecture Center and updated quarterly

Document owners: PVN PavanKumar and Anirban Majumdar (Head of OCTO, SAP SE)

 

 

The Big Picture: Why This Architecture Matters Now

For decades, enterprise software has been a system of record. It stores transactions, enforces rules, and tells you what happened. But it cannot tell you why decisions were made, what alternatives were considered, or what to do next. That reasoning has always lived in people — scattered across emails, meetings, and tribal knowledge.

SAP believes AI makes it possible to build a system of context on top of the system of record. A connected layer where enterprise data, process knowledge, and decision history are linked together so that AI agents can reason across them — not just retrieve from them.

The scale of the opportunity is significant. AI is expected to add trillions of dollars to global GDP over the coming decade. Enterprise AI software is projected to grow into hundreds of billions in annual value. More importantly, the nature of AI-driven work is changing: tasks that take minutes today are on a path toward running for a full human workday autonomously within a few years.

 

Key Insight from the Paper

"In enterprise AI, context is the decisive advantage."

Frontier models are becoming commoditized — every vendor can access the same leading models.

What varies is the context those models reason over.

SAP has five decades of business processes, master data, and domain knowledge.

That context is the strategic moat — and no model can replicate what it has never seen.

 

 

From AI-First to AI-Native: What Is the Difference?

This distinction is at the heart of the paper and worth understanding clearly because it changes how you think about what SAP products will look like in the coming years.

 

 

AI-First

In an AI-first world, AI is a feature added to an existing application. An AI assistant in your ERP can summarize an invoice or suggest a journal entry. But it is bounded by that application. It cannot see that the same supplier has open disputes in service management, delayed shipments in logistics, and a renegotiated contract in procurement — because those facts live in separate systems with no shared understanding.

SAP describes three barriers that keep AI-first limited: AI systems lack business and process context (leading to unreliable answers), they sit on disconnected systems without a shared data model, and they lack the governance needed to be accountable at scale.

AI-Native

AI-native solves all three. Software operates across landscape boundaries as a system of context. Every interaction feeds intelligence. Every correction becomes a learning signal. The system continuously improves. The delivery model shifts from software as a service to outcome as a service.

 

 

The Architecture: Four Layers Working Together

The AI-native North Star architecture is built on four reimagined layers. Together, they form what SAP calls the Cognitive Core — an enterprise intelligence system that integrates SAP's business data, process knowledge, and reasoning models to enable systems that learn and adapt.

 

 

  1. User Experience Layer

Today, users navigate between applications, follow predefined workflows, and manually piece together information across systems. In the AI-native model, users simply state their intent and the system assembles the experience around them.

The central component here is Joule Work — SAP's unified entry point that interprets intent across natural language, voice, and multimodal inputs. SAP describes five experience modes that Joule will support:

  • Discover — a personalized overview that learns over time
  • Conversations — ask, search, act, and get insights
  • Spaces — dynamic work environments assembled on the fly
  • Jobs — making agentic work traceable for business users
  • Develop — turning business intents into agentic solutions

 

Looking ahead, SAP also mentions Physical AI — Joule moving beyond the screen into robotics and smart glasses, bringing SAP business logic into the physical world.

  1. Process Layer

The process layer is where business logic is defined. In the AI-native model, applications become capability providers for agents rather than the primary executors of work. A procurement agent, for example, can orchestrate across ERP, sourcing, supplier management, and third-party systems as a single domain-aware participant.

Agents follow a reason-act-observe loop: they decompose goals, execute actions using tools, and refine based on results. SAP supports building solutions across a spectrum from fully deterministic apps to open-ended agents with broader autonomy.

All agent development comes together in Joule Studio — the unified design-time environment. Developers can go from business intent to code, following a flow from requirements document through specification to deployment.

  1. Foundation Layer (AI and Data)

This is where data and AI come together as the intelligent core. SAP describes this layer as evolving from a system of record to a system of context — where data, process knowledge, and decision history are connected and compounding.

Three components define this layer:

  • SAP Business Data Cloud — connects data from across the enterprise into a single governed landscape, with data products as the primary interface between SAP data and the agents, dashboards, and AI systems that consume it
  • SAP Knowledge Graph — the semantic backbone of the architecture. It links natural language inputs to SAP's structured metadata, opening access to tens of thousands of APIs and data models. When a user asks "show me overdue orders," the Knowledge Graph finds the right API, identifies correct parameters, and constructs an accurate query
  • Model Services — SAP-trained domain models (including RPT-1 for structured business data), plus access to third-party frontier LLMs through a generative AI hub

 

One standout insight from the paper: "Data was the moat of the last decade. Context is the moat of the next."

  1. Platform Layer

The platform layer is where applications, agents, and workflows run. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides composable services across data management, application development, integration, and security.

In the AI-native model, BTP evolves from hosting applications to running SAP-managed enterprise agents across the customer landscape. The platform provides:

  • Managed agent runtime with sandboxing, identity, memory, and observability built in
  • Agent harness — the governance layer that wraps models with enterprise controls
  • Agentic SDKs for building agents in any framework
  • Observability built on Open Telemetry covering agent execution end to end

 

A key insight from the paper: "The model reasons. The harness governs. The harness, not the model, determines the ceiling."

 

The Autonomous Enterprise: SAP's Vision for the Future

The Autonomous Enterprise is SAP's term for the destination this architecture is building toward. It describes enterprises that respond more quickly, operate more intelligently, and perform better by design — using AI assistants and agents to manage and execute processes across domains in one connected, adaptive system.

 

 

 

Element

What It Does

Joule

Central engagement layer bringing together data, workflows, and agents across all SAP systems and beyond

SAP Autonomous Suite

AI agents executing work across Finance, Spend, SCM, HCM, and Customer Experience

Industry AI Portfolio

Built-in vertical process knowledge, domain-specific data models, and regulatory logic

SAP Business AI Platform

Unifies SAP BDC, BTP, Business Transformation Management, and AI Foundation for enterprise-grade governance

RISE/GROW with SAP

Accelerated adoption path for the Autonomous Enterprise through agent-led toolchains

 

The Data Flywheel: Why Context Compounds

One of the most important conceptual contributions of the paper is the idea of the data flywheel. Within the four-layer architecture, data informs decisions, decisions improve processes, and better processes generate richer data. This is a self-reinforcing loop that creates a compounding advantage over time.

 

 

This is why SAP argues that the Autonomous Enterprise is not just a faster version of today's ERP. It is a qualitatively different kind of system — one that gets better with every interaction, every correction, and every resolved exception. The value compounds in a way that static software cannot.

 

 

Integration, Security, Ethics and Governance

Integration: From Connector-Driven to Intelligence-Driven

SAP describes integration itself as shifting from connector-driven to intelligence-driven. In the AI-native model, agents dynamically determine intent, select the right APIs, and orchestrate calls across systems — rather than relying on pre-built point-to-point connectors.

Two open protocols underpin this:

  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) — provides tool integration, enabling agents to discover and invoke capabilities across systems
  • Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol — supports multi-agent coordination, enabling agents built by different teams, partners, and customers to collaborate within governed boundaries

Security and Agent Identity

Agentic AI introduces new security risks that traditional frameworks were not designed to handle. Agents make autonomous decisions, access external tools, and operate across trust boundaries. SAP's response is to treat agents as first-class principals — they authenticate, receive authorizations, and are governed like any other enterprise actor.

SAP describes a three-tier AI defense architecture: a Foundation tier (zero-trust authentication, prompt-injection detection), an Automation tier (AI agents that scale security operations), and a Supervision tier (deterministic control agents that monitor and authorize other agents).

AI Ethics and Governance

The paper gives significant attention to AI ethics, treating it as a foundational cross-cutting concern rather than a compliance checkbox. SAP applies a structured AI ethics impact assessment to all use cases, classifying them by risk. High-risk use cases escalate to an AI ethics steering committee and, when necessary, to the SAP Executive Board.

SAP's ethics approach is aligned with UNESCO's Recommendation on the Ethics of AI and the OECD AI Principles. The paper is clear: when no ethically acceptable trade-off can be found, the AI system cannot proceed in that form.

 

 

The Ecosystem: Marketplace for Agents, Tools and Extensions

The AI-native architecture is designed to extend beyond SAP. The SAP AI Agent Hub serves as the central marketplace where agents, data products, and tools are published, evaluated, and composed into solutions. The full agent lifecycle is managed here — from planning and development through discovery, provisioning, observability, governance, and optimization.

Partners can build and publish domain-specific agents — logistics optimization, regulatory compliance, demand forecasting — with standardized metadata, quality metrics, and trust ratings. Customers can extend SAP-built agents with their own business rules, custom data sources, and industry-specific knowledge.

The result is a network effect: more agents → more capabilities → more builders → more value. The same platform dynamics that defined consumer technology are now being applied to enterprise intelligence.

 

 

Architecting the Quantum Era

The paper closes with a section on quantum computing — not as a near-term reality but as an architectural consideration SAP is already designing for. Quantum processing units (QPUs) will operate as mathematical coprocessors for complex optimization and simulation tasks, with outputs flowing back into deterministic workflows.

SAP describes a hybrid model where classical determinism, AI-enhanced cognition, and quantum probability work together within a trusted enterprise foundation — with post-quantum cryptography built in from the start.

 

 

Quick Reference: Key Terms from the Paper

If you are reading the full paper, these terms come up frequently. Here is a plain-language reference:

 

 

 

What This Means for SAP Customers and Professionals

The North Star Architecture is a vision document, not a product roadmap. But it signals clearly where SAP is heading and has several practical implications for organizations using SAP today.

 

Area

Implication for SAP Organizations

User Experience

Joule becomes the primary interface. Traditional Fiori apps remain but natural language and intent-driven UX will grow in importance

Process Design

Business processes will increasingly be described as agent capabilities rather than rigid workflow steps

Data Strategy

Data products and semantic data management become more important — garbage-in is even more costly when agents are reasoning over data

IT Architecture

Agent identity, governance, and observability need to be planned for early — not retrofitted

Skills

Understanding MCP, agent orchestration, context engineering, and semantic data modeling will be valuable

Governance

AI ethics and risk classification processes need to be established before agents are deployed in high-stakes workflows

entry.title: SAP Cloud Support Organization: A Guide to Roles and Responsibilities
entry.content:
Overview

In my years of engaging with SAP customers, I have consistently observed a recurring pain point: many customers, even after subscribing to SAP cloud services, remain unclear about which SAP personnel to contact and for what purposes. Unlike B2C interactions — where a consumer can simply open an app and reach a support agent — enterprise software is an entirely different landscape. SAP's organization is large and multifaceted, spanning numerous modules and specialized domains, which makes navigating the right contacts genuinely challenging.

This guide provides a clear breakdown of SAP's internal roles and responsibilities, enabling you to leverage SAP's resources more effectively.

Overview

To address the demands of cloud-era subscription models, SAP has built a multi-tiered customer support framework that spans the entire customer lifecycle. From pre-sales account executives to post-contract Onboarding Advisors and Customer Success Managers, through to delivery and operations roles such as ECS, TQM, and SAP Support, and extending to optional value-added services like Preferred Success and CAS — each role carries a distinct mandate, and all operate in coordinated collaboration to drive customer adoption and sustained value realization.

The subscription model inherently demands a more granular, continuous approach to customer support. From initial order placement through go-live to renewal, different roles must be engaged and orchestrated at each stage.

1. Sales / Industry Account Executive (IAE) — Included

Scope of Responsibility Responsible for ensuring customers procure the right solutions and services aligned to their business requirements. The IAE serves as the customer's primary point of contact at SAP throughout the entire cloud journey.

Engagement Phase Pre-sales through contract execution, and subsequently during change request phases.

Key Deliverables Contract execution, solution selection guidance, change order coordination.

Customer Interaction Model Engages directly with senior stakeholders and business decision-makers to ensure commercial agreements are properly structured and executed. In the absence of a CSM, the IAE may also coordinate and initiate the Onboarding process.

2. CSM / D-CSM (Customer Success Manager / Digital Customer Success Manager) — Included

Scope of Responsibility The CSM role is broadly consistent with its counterpart at other cloud vendors: it is designed to manage the customer lifecycle post-contract. It is important to distinguish this from the sales function — while the IAE is also involved across the full customer lifecycle, the sales focus is anchored to commercial milestones. Everything beyond that — product usage guidance, billing clarification, best practices, and internal resource coordination — falls within the CSM's remit.

That said, not every customer is assigned a CSM. CSM allocation is typically threshold-based, tied to subscription value within a given product line. To illustrate (figures are hypothetical): if a customer has invested €2M in SAP BTP and only €100K in SAP SuccessFactors, they would likely receive a dedicated BTP CSM but not a SuccessFactors CSM.

A note on Digital CSMs (D-CSMs): these individuals typically manage a portfolio of over one hundred accounts. While they perform the same functional role, the depth of product knowledge and service intensity they can offer is necessarily more limited than a dedicated CSM. If you encounter one, they remain a valid escalation path — they will route you to the appropriate internal resource.

Engagement Phase Post-contract, spanning the full subscription lifecycle — go-live, ongoing operations, and renewal.

Key Deliverables Internal resource coordination, customer satisfaction management, Onboarding and implementation consultant engagement, renewal facilitation.

Customer Interaction Model Regular engagement with customer leadership; coordination of internal SAP resources including Onboarding Advisors and implementation consultants; central point of orchestration when customers encounter escalations or complex challenges.

Note: In certain SAP documentation, the CSM role may also be referred to as E-CSP (Enterprise Cloud Success Partner). The S-CSP designation refers to a product line-specific variant.

3. Onboarding Advisor / Onboarding Consultant — Included

Scope of Responsibility Part of SAP's Customer Service Delivery (CSD) organization, Onboarding Advisors are product line specialists responsible for introducing customers to relevant services, guiding initial provisioning and activation, ensuring timely service delivery, and driving early customer satisfaction.

Engagement Phase Early post-contract period (the Onboarding phase), with primary impact in the weeks immediately following contract signature.

Key Deliverables Product onboarding orientation (how to submit tickets, manage S-users, register for webinars); provisioning walkthroughs for BTP, BDC, and AI-related services (BTP Cockpit, Integration Suite, data warehousing, HANA, SAP Build, etc. — typically 1–1.5 hours per topic); confirmation that customers can successfully access relevant services for development and consumption.

Customer Interaction Model Guidance and enablement delivered via a structured series of online sessions. Engagements may be initiated by the CSM, the sales team, or the customer directly; Onboarding Advisors also proactively reach out to customer IT managers. The engagement model is intentionally flexible. Post-go-live issues are subsequently handed off to other SAP resources (e.g., implementation consultants) coordinated by the CSM — Onboarding Advisors do not maintain ongoing tracking after this point.

4. ECS (Enterprise Cloud Services) — Included

ECS comprises three dedicated roles, primarily engaged during the delivery and operational phases of private cloud ERP environments. This function is principally oriented toward customers running SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud. If your questions relate to BTP — such as subaccount creation, identity provider integration, or SAP Analytics Cloud provisioning — ECS is not the right contact. For those topics, engage your SAP AE, CSM, or Onboarding Advisor.

Project Lead (PL)

Scope of Responsibility Responsible for ensuring SAP builds and delivers the system in accordance with what was sold and contractually agreed. Engaged during the system build phase, and re-engaged if changes or extensions are required subsequently.

Engagement Phase System delivery / build phase (time-bound engagement).

Key Deliverables On-scope system delivery, change request execution.

Customer Interaction Model Direct collaboration with the customer throughout the system build phase.

Client Delivery Manager (CDM)

Scope of Responsibility Owns the operational phase of the delivered system and serves as the single point of contact for all matters related to S/4HANA configuration and ongoing operations.

Engagement Phase System operations phase (long-term, ongoing).

Key Deliverables Operational coordination, issue escalation management.

Customer Interaction Model Primary ongoing contact for the operational phase; maintains continuous customer engagement.

Technical Service Manager (TSM)

Scope of Responsibility A seasoned SAP technical consultant who works in close partnership with the CDM and serves as the primary technical contact for all infrastructure and technology-related topics.

Engagement Phase System operations phase (long-term, ongoing).

Key Deliverables Technical issue resolution, infrastructure support.

Customer Interaction Model Addresses technical and infrastructure-related matters in close coordination with the CDM.

5. TQM (Technical Quality Manager) / Launch Advisor (LA) — Billable

Scope of Responsibility The Launch Advisor is a senior, multi-disciplinary expert — often described as a "black belt" — who helps customers plan and navigate each phase of their cloud journey. This includes coaching and upskilling customers and their organizations, proactively identifying risks and issues before they materialize, and supporting their resolution.

Engagement Phase Can be engaged at any point in the process for as long as needed; earlier engagement consistently yields better outcomes.

Key Deliverables Risk identification and mitigation, project planning guidance, organizational capability development.

Customer Interaction Model Procured on demand; delivers highly personalized, hands-on expert engagement. SAP's experience consistently shows that having a Launch Advisor is a material success factor for complex implementations.

Note: In other SAP product lines and service frameworks — particularly MaxAttention and ActiveAttention — the equivalent role is referred to as TQM (Technical Quality Manager).

6. SAP Support (Standard Support) — Included

Scope of Responsibility SAP Support handles product-specific issues: software bugs, user account lockouts, contact record updates, service outages, and similar incidents. They are not equipped to address business process questions or technical implementation consulting — their scope is product support. All interactions are managed through the formal case management process via SAP for Me.

Engagement Phase Throughout the entire product lifecycle — implementation, operations, and upgrade phases.

Key Deliverables Support case management, root cause analysis, technical guidance.

Customer Interaction Model Customers submit support cases via their S-user credentials on the SAP for Me portal.

7. Preferred Success — Billable

Scope of Responsibility A personalized, proactive value-added support offering designed for cloud customers. It accelerates time-to-value on cloud investments through proactive engagement, insight-driven recommendations, release guidance, targeted learning plans, and enhanced SLAs.

Engagement Phase Throughout the cloud solution lifecycle (post-go-live, ongoing operations).

Key Deliverables

  • Solution launch reviews and success checks
  • Adoption plans, quality reviews, and business case analysis
  • In-depth release guidance
  • Customized release plans to accelerate readiness and feature adoption
  • Targeted learning plans
  • Key user and administrator training
  • Enhanced support with elevated SLAs
  • Faster incident response and resolution of non-defect issues
  • Dedicated Customer Success experts and SAP subject matter expert access for complex topics

Customer Interaction Model Proactive partnership model with regular check-ins, expert consultations, and learning plan delivery. Extended tiers provide additional expert guidance, enhanced services, and adoption practice support.

Applicable Products: SAP Ariba, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Fieldglass, SAP Supply Chain Management, SAP BTP, and others.

8. CAS (SAP Cloud Application Services) — Billable

Scope of Responsibility Provides supplementary operational support and managed service subscriptions, with tailored offerings for specific industries such as utilities. Customers may procure CAS on demand for additional cloud application operations support.

Engagement Phase Operations phase (procured on demand).

Key Deliverables Cloud application operations support, industry-specific operational task execution.

Customer Interaction Model Upon subscription, the CAS team delivers dedicated operational support services to the customer.

9. COE Service (Center of Expertise) — Billable

Scope of Responsibility COE is SAP's capability-building and support collaboration framework for partners and customers. SAP recommends that partners establish a COE, supported by dedicated Support Manager and Support Advisor roles, to enable effective collaboration with SAP Support. The framework encompasses service delivery and quality management, certification and resource governance, and support process integration.

Engagement Phase Throughout the implementation and operations lifecycle.

Key Deliverables Support collaboration analytics dashboards (SAP for Me), case resolution optimization, project risk reduction, customer satisfaction improvement.

Customer Interaction Model Partners engage with SAP Support on behalf of customers through the COE mechanism, utilizing S-user credentials to create and manage support cases.

10. Additional Supporting Roles

Cloud Architect & Advisor (CAA)

Scope of Responsibility Collaborates with the sales team to validate order deliverability and prevent downstream surprises. Responsible for supporting the completion of the Customer Onboarding Form (COF) and facilitating the Sales-to-Delivery (S2D) handoff. Re-engaged for complex change requests to ensure commercial agreements are executable.

Engagement Phase Pre-sales through S2D handoff; re-engaged for complex change requests.

Partner Coaching / Partner Mentoring Services

Scope of Responsibility SAP provides dedicated Coaching (10 sessions of 30 minutes each, over 6 months) and Mentoring (1 hour per week, 13 sessions over 3 months) engagements for partners, covering functional solutions, configuration, technical topics, and project management. Applicable to S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, Ariba, CX, and other product lines.

Engagement Phase Partner implementation phase.

SAP MaxAttention / ActiveAttention

Scope of Responsibility Premium success services providing planning, assurance, and remote expert support for complex system landscapes, programs, and operations across the full solution lifecycle.

Engagement Phase Full lifecycle coverage, particularly suited to large and complex customer environments.

Role Comparison Summary

Role Engagement Phase Primary Responsibility Organizational Unit Customer Type

Sales / IAEPre-sales through contractSolution selection, contract executionSAP SalesAll customers
CSM / D-CSMFull post-contract lifecycleCustomer relationship management, resource coordination, renewalSAP Customer SuccessCloud subscription customers
Onboarding AdvisorEarly post-contractProduct onboarding, service provisioning, enablementSAP Customer SuccessNew cloud customers
ECS (PL / CDM / TSM)System delivery and operationsSystem build and delivery, operational coordination, technical supportSAP ECSMid-to-large cloud customers (S/4HANA, etc.)
TQM / Launch AdvisorFull lifecycle (on demand)Risk identification, project coaching, organizational enablementSAP ECS / MaxAttentionComplex project customers
SAP SupportFull lifecycleCase management, technical support, patch deliverySAP SupportAll customers
Preferred SuccessFull cloud operations lifecycleProactive support, release guidance, learning plans, enhanced SLAsSAP ServicesCloud subscription customers (optional)
CASOperations phase (on demand)Cloud application operations, industry-specific tasksSAP CASSpecific industry cloud customers (optional)
COE ServiceFull implementation and operations lifecycleSupport collaboration, quality management, capability buildingPartner / Customer internalPartners and large customers (optional)

Customer Lifecycle and Role Collaboration

Lifecycle Stage Lead Role Collaborating Roles Key Activities

Pre-SalesSales / IAECAASolution selection, contract negotiation, COF completion
Contract & KickoffCSM / D-CSMOnboarding Advisor, CAAS2D handoff, Onboarding session scheduling, service provisioning
Implementation & Go-LiveECS (PL / CDM / TSM)Onboarding Advisor, Launch Advisor, SAP SupportSystem build and delivery, technical support, risk management
Operations & StabilizationCDM / TSM / SAP SupportPreferred Success, CAS, COEDay-to-day operations, case management, upgrades, continuous improvement
Renewal & ExpansionCSM / E-CSPSales / IAE, Preferred SuccessRenewal negotiation, subscription expansion, value review

Key Clarifications and Considerations

Role Boundaries The Onboarding Advisor's mandate is limited to the onboarding phase. Issues arising during implementation or post-go-live are escalated via the CSM to the appropriate resources, such as implementation consultants — Onboarding Advisors do not maintain ongoing ownership of these matters. Business process and technical implementation consulting should be directed to implementation partners (e.g., EY, IBM, Deloitte) or SAP's own COE delivery organization (available as an optional, billable service).

Role Availability Varies by Contract Type Not all roles are present in every engagement. ECS roles (PL, CDM, TSM) are primarily allocated to mid-to-large S/4HANA cloud customers. Launch Advisor and Preferred Success are both optional, purchasable value-added services.

The CSM as the Central Coordination Hub The CSM is the pivotal coordination role across the customer lifecycle. However, given that a single CSM typically manages multiple customer accounts, it is not feasible for them to maintain deep expertise across every product and handle every issue directly. Their primary value lies in orchestrating the right SAP internal specialists for each situation.

Preferred Success — Extended Tier Building upon the base subscription plan, the Extended tier offers additional expert guidance, enhanced service entitlements, and adoption enablement. It is currently available for SAP Ariba, Commerce Cloud, Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, SuccessFactors, Fieldglass, Supply Chain Management, and BTP.

entry.title: SAP客户支持角色详解看我这篇就够
entry.content:
长期以来，在与SAP客户接触的过程中，我发现很多客户，依然不知道在订阅了SAP的云服务以后，究竟要找SAP的哪些人来做哪些事。ToB的业务不像ToC，打开淘宝找客服就行了，SAP人员众多，涉及的模块和专业知识林林总总，十分容易混淆。今天看我这篇，让你清晰了解SAP公司原厂的员工职责划分，更好的利用SAP的资源。

概述

SAP 针对云时代的订阅模式，构建了一套覆盖客户全生命周期的多层次支持体系。从售前的销售团队，到签约后的 Onboarding 顾问、CSM，再到实施运营阶段的 ECS、TQM、SAP Support，以及可选购的增值服务（Preferred Success、CAS 等），各角色分工明确、协同配合，共同保障客户的成功采用与持续价值实现。

云时代的订阅模式对客户支持的周期有更高更细致的要求，从客户下单到上线，再到续签，都需要不同角色的参与与协调。

1. 销售 / 行业客户经理（Industry Account Executive, IAE）- 内含
  • 职责范围 负责确保为客户的业务需求订购正确的解决方案和服务；贯穿客户整个云旅程，是客户在 SAP 的主要联系人。
  • 服务阶段 售前阶段至合同签署全程，以及后续变更请求阶段。
  • 主要交付物 合同签署、解决方案选型建议、变更订单协调。
  • 与客户的互动方式 直接与客户高层及业务决策者沟通，确保商业协议落地；如无 CSM，也可由销售协调发起 Onboarding 流程。
2. CSM / D-CSM（Customer Success Manager, Digtal CSM) - 内含
  • 职责范围 CSM 与其他云公司的职责并无不同，角色的设计初衷是在客户签单以后负责客户的生命周期管理。注意这里和销售的不同，虽然销售也是客户的全周期，但是销售更关注和“钱”有关系的环节，至于产品使用说明，计费方式，最佳实践，协调SAP其他资源等则要统统交给CSM来完成。但是不是所有的客户都会有CSM，只有那些买够一定数额的产品才会分配对应产品的CSM，举个例子（价格仅仅是假设），如果你买了SAP的BTP产品，同时也购买了SAP的Successfactor，但是BTP你是重度用户买了200万欧元，Successfactor你只是最小量购买，只买了10万欧元，那么你作为客户会分配一个BTP的CSM，不会分配Successfactor的CSM。 这里要特别说明的是，你可能会遇到D-CSM，他们一个人要看上百客户，也会起到CSM的作用，不过他们对某类产品的知识和服务深度，不会到达CSM的层级，如果你遇到售后问题，找他们没有错，他们会帮你协调到对应的资源。
  • 服务阶段 合同签署后，贯穿整个订阅生命周期（上线、运营、续签）。
  • 主要交付物 协调 SAP 内部资源、提升客户满意度、协调 Onboarding 及后续实施顾问介入、推动续签。
  • 与客户的互动方式 定期与客户高层沟通；协调 Onboarding 顾问、实施顾问等内部资源；在客户遇到问题时统筹调度各方支持。

注：在部分文档中，CSM 也被称为 E-CSP（Enterprise Cloud Success Partner），S-CSP 则是专注特定产品线的专项 CSP。

3. Onboarding 顾问（Onboarding Advisor / Onboarding Consultant）- 内含
  • 职责范围 隶属于 SAP Customer Service Delivery（CSD）部门，是分散到各产品线的专家，负责讲解相关服务、带领客户开通并入门，保证按期交付服务，提高客户满意度。
  • 服务阶段 合同签署初期（Onboarding 阶段），重点在客户签约早期发挥作用。
  • 主要交付物 产品入门介绍（如何提交工单、如何管理 S 用户、如何注册网上研讨会）；BTP/BDC/AI 相关服务开通讲解（BTP Cockpit、集成套件、数仓、HANA、SAP Build 等，每项约 1~1.5 小时）；确保客户可以登录相关服务进行后续开发和使用。
  • 与客户的互动方式 通过一系列在线会议提供指导和赋能；可由 CSM 协调发起，也可由销售或客户自行发起，Onboarding顾问还会主动联系客户IT经理，形式十分灵活；客户项目实施过程中及上线后的问题，将由 CSM 协调其他部门（如实施顾问）跟进，Onboarding 顾问不再追踪。
4. ECS（Enterprise Cloud Services）- 内含

ECS 组织下设三个专属角色，主要在私有云ERP系统交付和运营阶段发挥作用。这一块儿主要针对买了SAP S/4HANA private cloud的客户，如果你遇到了BTP的问题比如如何进行子账户创建，如何和SAP身份认证系统打通，如何开通SAP分析云等，不要找他们，没用，找SAP AE/CSM/Onboarding顾问。

  • Project Lead（PL，项目负责人）

    • 职责范围 负责确保 SAP 按照销售和合同约定构建并交付系统；参与系统构建阶段，如后续需要变更或扩展，PL 会再次介入。
    • 服务阶段 系统交付/构建阶段（临时性参与）。[1]
    • 主要交付物 系统按约交付，变更请求落地。
    • 与客户的互动方式 在系统构建阶段与客户直接协作。

  • Client Delivery Manager（CDM，客户交付经理）

    • 职责范围 负责已销售和交付系统的"运营"阶段，是该阶段的单一联系人，协调 S/4HANA 设置和运营的各项事务。
    • 服务阶段 系统运营阶段（长期）。
    • 主要交付物 运营协调、问题升级处理。
    • 与客户的互动方式 作为运营阶段的主要联系人，持续与客户沟通。

  • Technical Service Manager（TSM，技术服务经理）

    • 职责范围 经验丰富的 SAP 技术顾问，与 CDM 紧密合作，是所有技术和基础设施相关主题的主要技术联系人。
    • 服务阶段 系统运营阶段（长期）。
    • 主要交付物 技术问题解决、基础设施支持。
    • 与客户的互动方式 处理技术和基础设施相关问题，与 CDM 协同工作。
5. TQM（Technical Quality Manager）/ Launch Advisor（LA）- 付费
  • 职责范围 Launch Advisor（LA）是经验丰富的多面手"黑带"专家，帮助客户规划和导航云旅程的各个阶段，辅导和培训客户及其组织，提前发现问题或风险，并协助解决。
  • 服务阶段 可在流程中的任何时间点介入，持续时间根据需要而定；越早介入效果越好。
  • 主要交付物 风险识别与规避、项目规划辅导、组织能力建设。
  • 与客户的互动方式 按需订购，提供高度个性化的专家陪伴式服务；SAP 经验表明，对于复杂项目，拥有 Launch Advisor 是重要的成功因素。

注：在 SAP 的不同产品线和服务体系中，类似角色也被称为 TQM（Technical Quality Manager），主要见于 MaxAttention/ActiveAttention 高级支持服务中。

6. SAP Support（标准支持）- 内含
  • 职责范围 简单来说，他们只负责处理产品相关的问题比如Bug，用户锁定，客户联系人更改，serivce宕机等，他们无法处理业务或者技术实施的顾问问题，并且方式是通过SAP for Me开工单的形式来解决。
  • 服务阶段 : 贯穿整个产品生命周期（实施、运营、升级阶段）。
  • 主要交付物 问题工单处理、根本原因分析、技术指导。
  • 与客户的互动方式 客户通过 S 用户在 SAP for Me 提交支持案例
7. Preferred Success（SAP 优选成功服务）- 付费
  • 职责范围 面向云客户的个性化、主动式增值支持服务，通过主动合作、洞察驱动的建议、版本指南、有针对性的学习计划和增强 SLA，帮助客户快速实现云投资价值。
  • 服务阶段 贯穿云解决方案的整个生命周期（上线后持续运营）。
  • 主要交付物
    • 解决方案启动检查和成功检查 采用计划、质量审核、业务案例分析
    • 深度版本指南 定制化版本计划，加快版本准备和功能采用
    • 有针对性的学习计划 关键用户和管理员培训
    • 高级支持和增强 SLA 更快的事件响应和非缺陷问题解决
    • 客户成功专家 SAP 主题专家支持，处理复杂议题
  • 与客户的互动方式 主动式合作伙伴关系，定期检查、专家咨询、学习计划推送；扩展版本提供更多专家指导、增强服务和功能采用实践帮助。

适用产品：SAP Ariba、SAP Commerce Cloud、SAP Sales Cloud、SAP Service Cloud、SAP SuccessFactors、SAP Fieldglass、SAP Supply Chain Management、SAP BTP 等。

8. CAS（SAP Cloud Application Services）- 付费
  • 职责范围 提供额外的支持和运营任务订阅服务，专为特定行业（如公用事业）设计，客户可按需订购以获得额外的云应用运营支持。
  • 服务阶段 系统运营阶段（按需订购）。
  • 主要交付物 云应用运营支持、特定行业运营任务。
  • 与客户的互动方式 客户订购后，由 CAS 团队提供专项运营支持服务。
9. COE Service（Center of Expertise，卓越中心服务）- 付费
  • 职责范围 COE 是 SAP 支持体系中面向合作伙伴和客户的能力建设与支持协作机制。SAP 建议合作伙伴建立 COE，通过支持经理（Support Manager）和支持顾问（Support Advisor）角色，实现与 SAP 支持的高效协作，涵盖服务交付与质量管理、认证与资源管理、支持流程集成等。
  • 服务阶段 贯穿项目实施和运营全周期。
  • 主要交付物 支持协作分析仪表盘（SAP for Me）、案例处理优化、项目风险降低、客户满意度提升。
  • 与客户的互动方式 合作伙伴通过 COE 机制代表客户与 SAP 支持协作，使用 S 用户代表客户创建支持案例。
10. 其他支持角色 

  • Cloud Architect & Advisor（CAA，云架构师与顾问）

    • 职责范围 与销售团队协作，确保订单可交付，避免后续意外；负责协助客户填写 Customer Onboarding Form（COF），支持销售到交付（S2D）的交接；对于复杂变更请求，CAA 会再次介入确保商业协议可落地。
    • 服务阶段 售前至 S2D 交接阶段；复杂变更请求时再次介入。

  • 合作伙伴教练服务 / 合作伙伴导师服务

    • 职责范围 SAP 为合作伙伴提供专属教练（10 场 30 分钟远程会议，6 个月内）和导师（每周 1 小时，3 个月共 13 场）服务，涵盖功能解决方案、配置、技术或项目管理问题，适用于 S/4HANA Cloud、SuccessFactors、Ariba、CX 等产品。
    • 服务阶段 合作伙伴项目实施阶段。

  • SAP MaxAttention / ActiveAttention

    • 职责范围 高级成功服务，为复杂系统架构、项目和运营提供规划和保障，提供远程专家支持，覆盖整个解决方案生命周期。
    • 服务阶段 全生命周期（尤其适合大型复杂客户）。

角色对比总览

角色 服务阶段 主要职责 归属部门 适用客户类型

销售 / IAE售前至签约解决方案选型、合同签署SAP Sales所有客户
CSM / D-CSM签约后全生命周期客户关系管理、资源协调、续签SAP Customer Success云订阅客户
Onboarding 顾问签约初期产品入门、服务开通、赋能SAP Customer Success新签云客户
ECS（PL/CDM/TSM）系统交付与运营系统构建交付、运营协调、技术支持SAP ECS中大型云客户（S/4HANA等）
TQM / Launch Advisor全程（按需）风险识别、项目辅导、组织赋能SAP ECS / MaxAttention复杂项目客户
SAP Support全生命周期工单处理、技术支持、补丁交付SAP Support所有客户
Preferred Success云运营全周期主动支持、版本指南、学习计划、增强SLASAP Services云订阅客户（可选购）
CAS运营阶段（按需）云应用运营支持、特定行业任务SAP CAS特定行业云客户（可选购）
COE Service实施与运营全周期支持协作、质量管理、能力建设合作伙伴/客户内部合作伙伴及大型客户（可选购）

客户生命周期与角色协作关系

生命周期阶段 主导角色 协作角色 关键活动

售前阶段销售 / IAECAA（云架构师）解决方案选型、合同谈判、COF 填写
签约/启动阶段CSM / D-CSMOnboarding 顾问、CAA合同交接（S2D）、Onboarding 会议安排、服务开通
实施/上线阶段ECS（PL/CDM/TSM）Onboarding 顾问、Launch Advisor、SAP Support系统构建交付、技术支持、风险管控
运营/稳定阶段CDM / TSM / SAP SupportPreferred Success、CAS、COE日常运营、工单处理、版本升级、持续优化
续约/扩展阶段CSM / E-CSP销售 / IAE、Preferred Success续签谈判、扩展订阅、价值回顾

关键说明与注意事项

  • 角色边界说明 Onboarding 顾问仅负责客户入门阶段，项目实施过程中及上线后的问题由 CSM 协调实施顾问等其他资源跟进，Onboarding 顾问不再追踪。业务或者技术实施咨询，应该交由合作伙伴partner（比如EY，IBM，德勤等）或者SAP原厂COE实施（可选购）。
  • 角色因合同类型而异 并非所有角色都会同时出现。例如，ECS 的 PL/CDM/TSM 主要服务于中大型 S/4HANA 云客户；Launch Advisor 和 Preferred Success 均为可选购的增值服务。
  • CSM 的协调核心地位 CSM是贯穿客户生命周期的核心协调角色，但由于一个 CSM 通常面对多个客户，无法对每个产品的细节和所有问题全权把控，因此需要协调 SAP 内部各专业资源。
  • Preferred Success 扩展版 在基础订阅计划之上提供更多专家指导、增强服务和功能采用实践帮助，目前适用于 SAP Ariba、Commerce Cloud、Sales Cloud、Service Cloud、SuccessFactors、Fieldglass、Supply Chain Management 和 BTP 等产品。

entry.title: Let’s Talk Data від SAP: розмови про реальну роботу з даними
entry.content:
Якщо ви — дата-аналітик, дата-інженер, дата-саєнтист або просто людина, яка цікавиться тим, як насправді влаштована робота з даними в бізнесі — це подкаст, який варто додати до своєї бібліотеки просто зараз.

Let's Talk Data — це регулярна серія подкастів від SAP про людей, які роблять дані корисними. Нові епізоди виходять постійно, охоплюючи широкий спектр тем: від управління даними та дата-архітектури до AI, машинного навчання та побудови дата-орієнтованих організацій. Як дані живуть у реальних організаціях — очима тих, хто будує, керує та використовує їх щодня.

Про що цей подкаст?
Зараз триває 10-й сезон, і його фокус — на людях, які рухають дата-індустрію вперед. Кожен епізод — це відверта розмова про реальний вплив ідей, щоденні рішення та виклики побудови дата-орієнтованих організацій.
Якщо ви стежите за тим, як змінюється роль даних у бізнесі, або просто шукаєте якісний англомовний контент для професійного розвитку — Let's Talk Data заслуговує на вашу увагу.

Один із нещодавніх епізодів добре ілюструє про що йдеться:  "How SAP Business Data Cloud is Transforming Data Science w/ Databricks and AI".
У ньому ведучий José Chicas, продуктовий маркетинг-менеджер SAP Business Data Cloud, розмовляє з Leona Hasani — продакт-менеджером SAP BDC, яка до переходу в продуктову роль кілька років працювала дата-саєнтистом. Leona не просто розповідає про продукт — вона знає, як виглядає ця робота зсередини, з усіма її труднощами та нюансами. Саме тому розмова виходить не рекламною, а справді корисною. У центрі епізоду — реальні больові точки, які знайомі кожному, хто працює з даними:

  • 60–70% часу — на підготовку даних
    Більша частина роботи дата-саєнтиста — це аж ніяк не побудова моделей. Це очищення даних, злиття таблиць, пошук пропущених значень, усунення дублікатів. І це може займати до 70% усього робочого часу.
    Чи звучить це знайомо?
  • Бізнес-контекст, який губиться по дорозі
    Ще одна больова точка — коли ви отримуєте таблицю, але не розумієте, що означають її атрибути, звідки вони і яку логіку в них закладено. Дані є, але довіри до них немає.

І саме тут розмова переходить від болю до рішення. Як SAP BDC разом із Databricks змінює цю реальність:

  • SAP Business Data Cloud + SAP Databricks
    Дата-саєнтисти отримують доступ до готових, перевірених дата-продуктів з вбудованим бізнес-контекстом — ієрархіями, KPI-визначеннями, сертифікованими наборами даних.
  • Zero-copy data sharing
    Окрема тема — обмін даними без їх фізичного копіювання. Це означає: реальний час, жодного дублювання, менші витрати і єдине джерело правди для всієї команди.
  • Реальний кейс: прогнозування затримок замовлень
    Щоб не залишатися на рівні теорії, епізод розбирає конкретний сценарій: як виробнича компанія може передбачати ризики затримки замовлень до того, як вони відбудуться — і що це означає для команди закупівель на практиці.

Чому це варто вашого часу
Цей подкаст про те, як насправді виглядає робота з даними, які рішення приймаються і чому. Кожен епізод — це можливість почути досвід людей, які будують дата-стратегії в реальних організаціях.
Якщо ви хочете бути в курсі того, куди рухається індустрія, — підписуйтесь і стежте за новими випусками. Теми змінюються, гості різні, але якість розмов залишається стабільно високою.

🔗 Корисні посилання
      🎬 YouTube-канал Let's Talk Data: youtube.com/@letstalkdatapodcast
      ▶️ Цей епізод на YouTube: дивитися тут
      🎧 Spotify: відкрити
      🍎 Apple Podcasts: відкрити
      📖 Дізнайтеся більше про цей епізод: стаття від  JoseChicas на SAP Community


Слухайте, діліться та залишайтеся в курсі — адже розуміти дані краще ніколи не пізно. 📊

entry.title: Celebrate 10 Years of SIT Barcelona: Join our Anniversary Photo Contest and Win Prizes!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

Can you believe it? SAP Inside Track Barcelona is turning 10 this year! 🎂🎉

A whole decade of learning, networking, sharing knowledge, and, most importantly, building an amazing community right here in Barcelona. From our very first edition to the most recent ones, it has been an incredible journey—and it’s all thanks to you.

To celebrate this major milestone, we want to look back at the moments that made these 10 years so special. And what better way to do that than through your eyes (and lenses)?

We are officially launching the SIT Barcelona 10th Anniversary Photo Contest!

🔍What are we looking for?

We want to dig into the archives. We are looking for photos from any of the past editions of SAP Inside Track Barcelona.

  • Did you take a great selfie with a speaker?

  • Do you have a shot of the networking coffee breaks or the after-event beers?

  • A picture of a packed room from years ago?

  • Even a funny, behind-the-scenes moment?

📝How to Participate (Rules)

Participating in the contest is super easy, and you can increase your chances by sharing as many memories as you want! Here is what you need to do:

  1. Be a member: You must be subscribed to the official SAP Community Barcelona Group. If you aren't yet, just click the "Join Group" button!

  2. Post your memories: Leave a comment below this blog post attaching your old photos from past SIT Barcelona events.

  3. No limits: Feel free to post a single comment or as many separate comments/photos as you like. Every unique photo counts as a new entry!

🏆Prizes & Winners

What's a contest without rewards? We will have exclusive prizes for the best, most nostalgic, or funniest photos!

The winners will be selected by our jury, and the official prizes will be handed out live during this year's SIT Barcelona event. Make sure you are there to collect yours!

Let’s make this anniversary unforgettable!

Start digging through your photo galleries today. Let’s show the world the incredible spirit of the SAP Barcelona community over the last 10 years.

 See you at SIT Barcelona!

Organizing team:
@Lluis , @ecastella @starverson @saquibus , Mireia Bubí & @david_ruizb

entry.title: Welcome to the SAP Orlando Community! Let’s Connect, Learn, and Grow Together
entry.content:
Hey Orlando Community!

I'm excited to join the community and introduce myself to all of you! As your curator of this group, I’d love to share a little about who I am, what you can expect from me, and my motivation behind organizing meaningful and engaging events for all of you.

😃About Me: I'm Fernando Lino, with over 25 years of experience working with SAP and digital transformation initiatives across industries. I’m passionate about bridging the gap between technology and business value—turning ideas like AI and intelligent enterprises into real-world execution. Outside of work, I enjoy connecting with communities, mentoring professionals, and exploring how technology can truly transform lives. Hosting local events is important to me because I believe strong communities accelerate learning, innovation, and meaningful connections beyond the corporate environment.

🤔What to Expect: I’ll be organizing a variety of local events tailored to our community’s interests—from deep-dive technical sessions to executive-level discussions, as well as networking opportunities that bring people together. I’m also always open to your ideas and feedback to make our community even more relevant, practical, and engaging.

🤗Let’s connect: I’d love to get to know each of you better and hear your thoughts on what makes a great event. Feel free to reach out here on the community platform or connect with me on LinkedIn.

Looking forward to meeting you all!

Best Regards,
Fernando Lino

entry.title: Missão Cumprida : Conclusão da 1º Academia Introdução ABAP na Comunidade CWB
entry.content:
Olá, SAP Community!

É com imensa alegria e sensação de dever cumprido que compartilhamos a conclusão bem-sucedida do Treinamento Introdutório em ABAP (SAP). Foram 20 horas (5 sábados) de muito aprendizado, troca de experiências e atividades práticas, fortalecendo a nossa comunidade local.  Como mencionado pelo nosso querido instrutor Madruga : "Quem conhece Curitiba sabe que acordar cedo e sair de casa em sábados frios e chuvosos exige muita coragem e determinação. Todos estão de Parabéns!"  

Esta foi a nossa primeira turma piloto, voltada aos estudantes da Escola Politécnica da PUCPR, resultado de uma parceria entre a CHpro Solutions, PUCPR  e a SAP Community Curitiba. Nosso grande intuito foi introduzir o ecossistema SAP aos universitários, proporcionando uma base sólida para lançá-los ao mercado de trabalho. Ver o engajamento, a dedicação e a vontade de aprender de cada participante nos dá ainda mais certeza de que estamos no caminho certo. Que esta seja apenas a primeira de muitas turmas e o início de uma jornada promissora para todos os futuros profissionais SAP que passaram por essa experiência.

Falando sobre jornada, essa foi incrível! Partimos do zero absoluto, entendendo a fundo o ecossistema SAP ERP. Muito além da teoria, compartilhamos vivências do dia a dia e situações que só a prática ensina. O foco não foi apenas o conhecimento técnico, mas também a bagagem de mercado de cada instrutor, trazendo cases reais para a sala de aula.

Colocamos a "mão na massa" no ambiente real de desenvolvimento, abordando conceitos essenciais de organização de pacotes, mandantes, gerenciamento de change requests e transporte de objetos entre ambientes, além de conceito de modelagem de dados e padronização de codificação ABAP. Também desmistificamos o Dicionário de Dados SAP, explicando na prática tabelas, visões, domínios, elementos de dados e as principais transações que auxiliam (e muito!) o nosso dia a dia. Ao final, focamos no desenvolvimento de relatórios, utilização de seleções e funções, debug e avaliação de bugs (ou "erro ABAP", como prefere o nosso instrutor Madruga, rs...), sempre aplicando as melhores práticas da linguagem. O resultado? Com certeza, ficou um gosto de "quero mais"!
